source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The star on the right is formed from 12 identical equilateral triangles. The length of the perimeter of the star is $36 \mathrm{~cm}$. What is the length of the perimeter of the shaded hexagon? <image1>

asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image file and registry_passed=true
multi_step: question_text includes multiple linked quantities/structures: a star, 12 identical equilateral triangles, total perimeter 36 cm, and a target shaded hexagon perimeter
solving likely requires at least two stages: infer a unit side length from the star perimeter, then compute the hexagon perimeter from that unit
the task combines geometric structure identification with arithmetic rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric result ('$18 \mathrm{~cm}$')
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling exact answer matching
the expected solution path is a count/geometry computation that is in principle checkable
